text:
Are you happy with your job? "
""
"Because, regardless of whether the answer is "yes" or "no", you should be job hunting (sort of)."
""
"Let me explain..."
""
"Most of us suck at job hunting. "
""
"Not because we lack talent or ambition, but because we treat it like a chore instead of a skill."
""
"Job hunting is one of the few critical life skills we only practice every few years. "
"Unlike daily skills we naturally improve through repetition, job hunting sits dormant until desperation strikes. "
"Then we rush to "get a job" rather than mastering the craft of landing the right opportunity."
""
"The feedback loop is broken too. "
"Employers rarely tell you why you didn't make the cut, so you're left guessing what went wrong. "
"Without feedback, there's no growth. "
"Without practice, there's no mastery."
""
"Here's the game-changer: Stop treating job hunting as an isolated event. "
""
"Instead, weave its core skills into your daily professional life:"
"⚫️ Practice articulating your value regularly, not just when updating your resume"
"⚫️ Build relationships before you need them"
"⚫️ Document your wins as they happen"
"⚫️ Seek mentorship and feedback consistently"
""
"Fold these habits into regular self-advocacy: whether you're gunning for a promotion, seeking a mentor, or just growing professionally."
""
"That's why the best time to hone your job-hunting skills is right when you don't need them.

Met some excellent folks at the Startup Fair at Harvard Innovation Labs this past Friday. We love these events because looking for interns doubles up as talking to potential users! "
""

""
"We want YOU to help us build the future of career growth at The ApplyAI, so come join the fun!

🥰 “This helps me feel more confident about my experience and the value I bring to any role”"
""
"🙏 It’s always a great feeling to hear someone say nice things about your product, but this one really hits home"
""
"😫 I know so many talented and capable people who *dread* interviews - who feel like they're never prepared enough to succeed. I used to be one of them!"
""
"👊 That’s why I’m so excited that people love the Interview Game Plans generated by The ApplyAI! It includes everything from the skills you should emphasize to the questions you can expect. Our AI even throws in some encouragement to lift your spirits."
""
"❗️ This feature is still in beta, so shoot me a message on LinkedIn if you have an interview coming up. I’ll personally onboard you and generate a free Interview game plan for the 3 jobs you’re most excited about.

There are some great suggestions for job seekers here, especially as a new generation prepares to enter the workforce. "
""
"Networking is always going to be important throughout your career, which is one reason I resonated with the advice contained herein. "
""
"Even when you are at a job, you should constantly be working on your interpersonal skills to build strong relationships with others, as these relationships can help you in a number of ways. "
""
"Related to this is the importance of self-awareness and self-reflection. "
""
"By understanding where you do well and where you don't, what makes you happy and what doesn't, you can begin to find room for pivots and a planned trajectory that tells you where you need to go. "
""
"#NewGrads #JobSearch #Career #PersonalGrowth

🎯 The *Real* Strategic Networker's Workflow (Because Random Connection Requests Don't Work!)"
""
"I've reviewed hundreds of networking approaches, and here's the uncomfortable truth: Most professionals are sabotaging relationships before they even start."
""
"Why? Because they treat networking like a numbers game instead of a strategic investment."
""
"Here’s what you should be doing:"
""
"1.⁠ ⁠Collect Vital Intel🔍"
"(Because surface-level research = surface-level relationships)"
"•⁠ ⁠Map your targeting strategically"
"•⁠ ⁠Study their career trajectory"
"•⁠ ⁠Identify genuine conversation starters"
"•⁠ ⁠Understand their industry challenges"
""
"2.⁠ ⁠Open with Confidence 💫"
"(First impressions last forever in professional relationships)"
"•⁠ ⁠Design outreach that proves you've done homework"
"•⁠ ⁠Reference specific work impacts"
"•⁠ ⁠Demonstrate informed curiosity"
"•⁠ ⁠Keep your ask small and specific"
""
"3.⁠ ⁠Cultivate a Relationship 🌱"
"(Value first, ask later - this is where trust is built)"
"•⁠ ⁠Position yourself as an engaged learner"
"•⁠ ⁠Share relevant industry insights"
"•⁠ ⁠Maintain meaningful touchpoints"
"•⁠ ⁠Focus on mutual growth"
""
"⚠️ Key Insight: Rushing to ask for favors before building rapport kills professional relationships, period. "
""
"Think about it: Would YOU help someone who clearly hasn't taken time to understand your work?"
""
"What aspects of networking feel particularly challenging? "
""
"Would sharing some real-life examples or critiquing past messages from real people be helpful?"
""
"Share and comment below for more such content!"
""
"#StrategicNetworking #ProfessionalGrowth #CareerDevelopment #NetworkingTips #CareerspanCareerspanCareerspan

The marginal cost of bullsh*t is now $0 — and it’s going to wreck the hiring market if we don’t do something."
""
"Every week I hear talent leaders bemoan: our inboxes are overflowing with seemingly “perfect” candidates who, when interviewed, leave much to be desired."
""
"Gartner projects that by 2028, one in four applicants will be fake."
""
"That's because job seekers have given up on the myth of “hiring meritocracy”. "
""
"After all, would you continue to spend months in ATS purgatory knowing that a few fabricated metrics and strategically placed keywords would at least get you a screening call?"
""
"A sliver of a chance is better than none at all. Hell, I don’t blame them one bit."
""
"When there’s no incentive for honesty and the measures of quality aren’t holistic, fabrication becomes rational—especially when “perfection” is just a few prompts away."
""
"Unfortunately, employers are going to react like they always have:"
"⚫️ Doubling down on proxies like pedigree and not genuine signals of talent and personality"
"⚫️ Adding more hoops for candidates to jump through"
"⚫️ Instituting background checks for their background checks"
""
"All while knowing, in their heart of hearts, that these are bandaids on a gunshot wound."
""
"Saying “Death to the résumé” isn’t rhetoric—it’s a warning. "
""
"We need evidence-rich, human-centric signals that illuminate how people think and what they add to a team, not just what a parser can keyword-match.

Got another fascinating job recommendation today: Music Central Assistant at Endeavor."
""
"Their algorithm's logic? Flawless."
""
"I exist in New York. They exist in New York. I was once in a team. They have a team."
""
"My decade in career coaching? Irrelevant."
""
"My complete lack of passion for working in the music industry? Details!"
""
"This isn't even uniquely bad. LinkedIn once suggested I'd be "perfect" for entry-level social media."
""
"I can only assume this is because I'm below the age of 75 and don't live in a cave. "
""
"Every platform plays keyword bingo. "
""
""You're interested in Strategy, you say? Would you prefer Chief Strategy Officer at Land O' Lakes or SEO Strategy Specialist at Lockheed?" "
""
"What sucks is that Music Central Assistant is probably perfect for someone out there and they almost certainly didn't get this posting (though they definitely got the Lockheed one)."
""
"These platforms have no f*ckin clue who you are."
""
".....But what if they did?"
""
"Not just 50 keywords they forced you to vomit out during onboarding. "
""
"I mean your values. Your deal-breakers. What makes YOU tick. And the context that informs whether you're applying to Disney or Deloitte. "
""
"That's why we're building Careerspan to be different. "
""
"We're coaching-driven because that is, objectively, the best way to understand a person and their goals--in all their messy and nuanced fullness. "
""
"So if you want a job recommendation system that gives enough of a shit to remember more than just your name, sign up at www.mycareerspan.com."
""
"Because the difference between matching keywords and matching humans makes all the difference in the world."
""
"Drop your best worst job rec below 👇

Humans use AI to spam applications."
"Companies use AI to filter out spam."
""
"It's a situation so tragic, Greek playwrights would call this "a bit much""
""
"So let me break it down Barney style for y'all. "
""
"Auto-apply is like me on the dance floor: it has a *very* limited set of moves and by jove is it going to use all of them."
""
"Swap "managed" for "spearheaded.""
"Guess that you "increased engagement by 25%." (why is it always 25%?)"
"Make up something so generic, the pharma industry wants to ban it"
""
"That's it. That's their whole schtick."
""
"It's as futile as trying to elevate a Ramen dinner."
"You can change the flavor packet."
"Add some frozen vegetables."
"Throw in some cheese if you're a nutcase like me."
"But when it's all said and done, that's still ramen."
""
"Because they don't really know you. And they don't care to as long as you'll pay them $50 a month and feel good that "number go up""
""
"Meanwhile, your *actual* career?"
"That's a walk-in fridge full of prime ingredients."
"The time you saved a product launch from disaster."
"Building a following from 500 to 5,000 during a brand crisis."
"The junior colleagues you mentored who all got promoted."
""
"Real stories. Real numbers. Real impact."
""
"Valuable context that auto-apply can't do jacksh*t without. "
""
"It's making instant ramen while your Michelin-worthy ingredients sit in the back--untouched, forgotten, and about to rot anytime. "
""
"Instead, it's the same, sad noodles hundreds of times a day."
"And hoping the hiring manager happens to be really, really hungry."
""
"So do yourself a massive favor: take stock of your career inventory."
""
"Because then, you'll really be cooking with gas!

🚩 Let's call it what it is -- there's a lot of BS jobs out there."
""
"Titles like "Chief Happiness Engineer" and "Digital Transformation Sherpa""
""
"Spin like "thrives in ambiguity" when the rest of the JD screams "we don't know what you'll be doing!""
""
"And the classic "competitive salary". Competitive with what? The poverty line?"
""
"Whether intentional or not, you should be aware of red flags in any job to which you apply. "
""
"That's why we built a BS Job Detector. "
""
"Just drop in the job description, and we'll highlight:"
"⚫️ Potential role misrepresentations"
"⚫️ Cultural red flags"
"⚫️ Unrealistic expectations "
"⚫️ Compensation Concerns"
"⚫️ Key clarifying questions to ask during interviews"
""
"It's totally free - no login or credit card needed. "
""
"Because if your dream job is actually a nightmare, it's best to know before you even apply!"
""
"🔗 Link in the comments. "
""
"Please share and repost for visibility and help out your peers who are job seeking!

If I've said it once, I've said it a thousand times... "
""
"👏 Energy and confidence management are the absolute most underrated part of job hunting"
""
"Look: you are not a machine for turning hopes and dreams into ATS-compliant resumes. "
""
"🫂 You are a person participating in a draining process that certainly won't let you rest if you don't give yourself some grace. "
""
"I know that's not easy -- I've been in that place of alternating between maniacally applying for jobs and dreading even opening my laptop as my self-esteem ticks lower with each unrequited workday application. "
""
"👌 That's why I love this post from Kim Wilkes."
""
"Not *just* because of the Careerspan signal boost (which is always appreciated!)"
""
"👉 But because she's sharing a powerful reminder that you'll go further and feel better if you punctuate your job search with activities that are grounding and rejuvenating.

I always thought "builder" would remain an unfulfilled ambition."
""
"Much like "stand-up comedian" and "starting forward for Arsenal FC" (don't laugh)."
""
"Turns out I may have a lot more of that first one in me than I thought, and it's thanks to Zo Computer!"
""
"Suffice it to say, for a non-technical founder like myself, Zo has been the absolute biggest unlock. "
""
"Zo has single-handedly replaced ChatGPT, Notion, n8n, and like 3 other products for me."
""
"And if you're even marginally tech-y, it could be a gamechanger for you as well. "
""
"That's why I'm chuffed to be speaking at their Builder Happy Hour with AI Circle at South Park Commons on Nov 5th. "
""
"You'll hear the sagely Ben Guo drop vibe coding wisdom for the ages, and I'll cap off the talk by demoing some of the cooler use cases I've built out. "
""
"Don't miss out. Event link is in the comments.

Out with the Old, in with the Niche

Out with the Old, in with the Niche

Created on 2020-04-17 12:20

Published on 2020-04-17 12:36

Change is the only constant on the internet, and this time, it’s coming for today’s social media behemoths. The once unquestionable dominance of companies like Facebook and Twitter has already begun to fade. For years now, they have been bleeding fringe users and struggling to acquire new ones. 

Millennials and Gen Zers are especially wary. They are far savvier online, more concerned by issues like privacy, and generally inclined to support companies that operate ethically. Instead, such users are increasingly turning to platforms whose communities are oriented around a particular interest, goal, or principle. I believe that this shift marks a new era in social networking—a growing rejection of the status quo and embracing of crowdculture. 

Currently, big players like Facebook are pursuing an expansive growth strategy focused on providing a broad array of services and features. Facebook, in particular, has made an effort to position itself as a one-stop-shop, not only for one’s social media needs but also for “IRL” purposes such as payment. This generalist nature and broad appeal, however, has its downsides: it does many things, but only a few them especially well. 

This overabundance of features, many of which do not operate effectively on mobile devices, has led to a convoluted platform that can charitably be described as a mess. By contrast, niche networks have taken a different route by offering specialized and well-defined value propositions. The evidence would suggest that this approach appeals to younger users, who are already used to splitting their engagement over several services. 

We need only look at the tremendous range of niche social networks to understand their appeal and explosive growth. There are established names like Pinterest and Tinder and newcomers like Tapped, a geosocial networking service for beer lovers, and Houzz, a social website about home decorating ideas. These communities are oriented around singular purposes—be it a profession or industry, hobby, or even shared experience—and designed to foster deeper connections. By contrast, on the big social sites, a person’s network is often so big that there’s no real sense of community. 

These networks also benefit from a clean slate as compared to Facebook and Twitter. These tech giants are regularly in the news for all the wrong reasons—from privacy violations to the treatment of their staff. Younger users care about whether the company they subscribe to is “problematic.” They are also more likely to seek out alternatives to mainstream brands with stronger community-oriented practices. For instance, despite weathering criticism over the years, Reddit has generally been responsive to the needs of its users—even if its response wasn’t to everyone’s satisfaction. 

Perhaps the ultimate proof is that these Goliaths of social networking are running scared is the fact that they’re very much hedging their bets. Facebook, for instance, made a conscious decision to limit integration between its primary brand and subsidiaries like Whatsapp and Instagram. As a result, they have avoided the dilution of their unique selling points and negative associations with their parent company. Indeed, these multi-billion- dollar brands are evidence of the tremendous growth potential of niche social networks. 

In many ways, these niche social networks reflect a change in what we want out of our online lives. Most of us will probably remain on mainstream platforms for the foreseeable future, but I believe our usage will increasingly reflect who we are and how we define ourselves. It’ll skew towards intimate networks that enable its users to share experiences and ideas in an authentic and personal way. Our various online presences will mirror who we are on the inside, not just how we want to present ourselves on the outside. You may not be able to do everything on niche social networks, but you’ll undoubtedly get what you came for.

I have exciting news for prospective MBAs looking to enter the world of management consulting upon graduation. McKinsey & Company has announced two fantastic programs that can help make that happen for you - McKinsey Early Access and Inspire: Black, Hispanic & Latino in Business, our diversity program."
""
"I can attest to the immense quality of my peers who have gone through these programs and the corresponding benefit it has had for their careers. I can also vouch for the fair and broadminded recruiting practices at McKinsey, so don't count yourself out before taking the time to learn more. "
""
"Should you have any questions about the Firm, our Marketing & Sales practice, or joining the New York office, I would be delighted to have a chat or, indeed, connect you with others who are equally well-versed in other offices and specialties."
""
"Please review the details of both programs below and keep their respective deadlines in mind. Best of luck!"
""
"2022 McKinsey Early Access:"
"McKinsey Early Access introduces you to McKinsey and consulting before you start business school. As part of the program, you will connect with peers in the US and build relationships with our consultants in advance of the fall recruiting season, start of classes and clubs, etc. McKinsey Early Access is inclusive to all backgrounds and helps support you as you start your MBA experience. We are particularly excited to meet problem solvers who are entrepreneurial, creative, and eager to participate in a close-knit, diverse, mentorship-focused community. "
""
"The application deadline is May 20, 2022. "
""
"You can find out more about the program on our website: https://lnkd.in/dW3ir5ni "
" "
"2022 Inspire: Black, Hispanic & Latino in Business:"
"We invite you to apply to Inspire, a two-day in-person event designed to support the professional development of underrepresented groups in leadership roles. Attendees who would like to pursue consulting careers will have the opportunity to jumpstart their McKinsey relationships and recruiting journey. At Inspire, we want you will feel at home, included, and comfortable in asking questions!  Inspire will introduce incoming MBA students in the United States who identify as Black, African-American, Hispanic, Latino/Latina, and/or Native American to our people and consulting. Attendees will hear examples of how we strive to make our firm—and our clients and society at large—more inclusive and equitable. Candidates will also get to meet like-minded peers and McKinsey colleagues in an intimate format. Inspire attendees who are interested in career opportunities at McKinsey will be invited to a special series of interview preparation and connectivity sessions throughout the year. Learn more about the program here: https://lnkd.in/dZx_j_zw. "
""
"Applications are accepted on a rolling basis until May 6th. To apply, visit https://lnkd.in/dt72jcxW

A brief update for my wonderful peers and colleagues on LinkedIn:"
""
"⏲️ What I’ve been up to"
"I’ve been growing The ApplyAI with my amazing co-founder Logan Currie."
""
"We’re building an AI career companion that’ll help you land your next big opportunity (more on this soon!) but I first wanted to catch everyone up. "
""
"🛫 The origin story"
""
"My first real job was in admissions and career coaching. I grew to love it, and I prided myself on the strong working relationships I built with clients. "
""
"The whole job boils down to two things: Hoining in on the standout things about my clients and helping them convey those highlights in a standout way. "
""
"After 5 years though, I grew restless and sought out new challenges. I loved deeply impacting people's lives, but since machines couldn't coach people at the time, I was never able to do so at scale. "
""
"🚀 How it started"
""
"Fast forward to November 2022, and the commercial launch of ChatGPT got me thinking. "Can Generative AI be used to crack that previously uncrackable nut: coaching at scale?""
""
"I didn't have the answer, but I knew who could help me find out. So, I did what any normal person would do: I prepared a 1000-word memo (read: screed) that sketched out the fundamentals of what we're building today. Then, without batting an eyelid, I sent it to Logan along with an emphatic “we NEED to talk”. The rest, as they say, is history. "
""
"👏 Why we’re in it to win it"
""
"Despite some bumps along the way, our conviction has never been higher. There’s a lot of “pain” being felt by job and promotion seekers right now - it’s harder than ever to get noticed and more competition than ever. "
""
"Why? Most people don’t know how to advocate for themselves. Those do usually benefitted from socioeconomic privilege or innate talent that eased their path. "
""
"We want to give everyone the benefits of a great career coach at a fraction of the cost, which is why we’re all in on this idea!"
""
"🙏 An ask and an offer"
""
"I want to end with an ask and an offer for my LinkedIn community. Both are optional. Either will be well received!"
""
"🗣️ The Ask: if you have strong feelings about jobs, career building, or recruitment and you’re open to sharing your thoughts, please DM me! I absolutely want to hear from you!"
""
"🎁 The Offer: If you or anyone you know needs help landing their next opportunity, put them in touch with me! I’ll personally onboard them onto the tool and give them a career coaching session. Book my time here: https://bit.ly/46TlKmw.

Ever felt your dream job was completely out of reach? "
"Ever felt completely lost while preparing for it? "
"Ever thought your prep wouldn’t make a difference? "
""
"At the start of my second year of the MBA, I felt all that and more when I decided to apply to McKinsey. I was totally out of my depth because I hadn’t recruited for consulting the previous year, so there was a lot of catching up to do and not much time to do it. "
""
"So I did the only thing I could: I put in dozens of hours to learn everything I could about casing and their interview process and catalog it in painful detail. The byproduct was an only *slightly* intense set of notes that I studied religiously. "
""
"Spoiler alert: I got the job! "
""
"What’s more, those notes that I had shared casually with some classmates became a big hit: they’ve racked up 1600+ views since September 2021 (see screenshot). That’s a lot considering Johnson only has about 200 people applying for consulting jobs across both years. "
""
"In retrospect, it makes total sense why I ended up enjoying getting through that interview process - it’s the same reason why I started The ApplyAI! Getting a great job is far from straightforward, and my team and I are planning to change that. "
""
"That’s why I’m here to share that same resource with LinkedIn. Here’s the McKinsey version (http://bit.ly/3NX5wST) and here’s a Firm-agnostic version (https://bit.ly/48rAbA1) for people looking at places besides the Firm. Please note that these are *supplementary* resources; they aren’t intended to build your capabilities from scratch. Think of them as an additional perspective on casing or behavioral interviews."
""
"If you were one of the people these notes helped, I’d love for you to leave a comment or repost for visibility! "
""
"For everyone else, follow us at The ApplyAI, sign up for our beta waitlist at www.theapply.ai, and watch this space for the even more exciting announcement that’s coming soon. My co-founder Logan and I think that’s wild that job hunters, particularly fresh grads, are expected to just “figure it out” without real help. We’re here to change that. "
""
"Finally, a huge thanks to my peers at Johnson who helped me get one of the biggest opportunities of my life. Here's a non-exhaustive list: Andrew Shin Sebastian Rodriguez Goumashvili Kevin Wang Madhuri Mehta Jacob Lehman Surina Shahani Uma Rajeswari Somireddi Thomas Stevenson Tarun Johnson Deepanshu Mehta José Enrique Gutiérrez Montaño. The camaraderie is what makes our program special, and I’m so grateful to have had the firsthand experience.

https://lnkd.in/e9uZ4JRC"
""
"These cuts are neither unique nor surprising. The AI genie is out of the bottle, and information-centric responsibilities and the workers that perform them will be the first on the chopping block. "
""
"LLMs - in my experience - are extremely efficient when given the right prompts and well-scoped-out data, and unfortunately for white collar workers, they can make a single well-trained person just as effective as several non-AI-enabled individuals. "
""
"The good news? AI can't match up to your soft skills. If there's one thing I've learned from Logan Currie, it's that you need to proactively find and cultivate your competitive advantage. Ironically, in a world full of AI, that's usually how well you work with other humans. "
""
"For many of us, that means finding ways to contribute to our teams/the bottom line in ways that go beyond our literal output on Excel or PowerPoint (and I say that as an ex-consultant). "
""
"Can you decompose a difficult problem into easy-to-digest components? Can you help your teammates do their job better with effective interventions? "
""
"I realize that for a lot of us, these contributions are on the periphery of things we were hired for. Unfortunately, these factors are going to matter a lot more than utilization or raw output sooner rather than later. "
""
"

"Whatever value they provide for weary job seekers, it’s worth noting that spray-and-pray [AI tools] don’t address the larger issue that prevents many applicants from landing a job” "
""
"Aki Ito really got to the heart of the matter in their Business Insider article on AI Job Search Bots: there’s an applicant arms race going on, where people are applying to more jobs than ever - and by all accounts, hearing back less and less. "
""
"Employers have had an overabundance of candidates long before these spray-and-pray tools emerged - there were an average of 250 applicants per corporate opening in 2023. I wouldn’t be surprised to see that number double in 2024."
""
"So sure, you could splurge on one of these tools, but if you can’t effectively communicate how great of a candidate you are in the first place, it won’t matter how many places you apply to: you’ll be invisible to employers who can’t see the best “you”. "
""
"I call this the “self-advocacy skill gap”.  Basically, how good is someone at putting themselves forward as a great candidate? This can be through their materials, their networking outreach messages, or interview answers. "
""
"In my experience, people are, at most, okay at self-advocacy – they can’t translate the great things about themselves into messaging that’s appealing and accessible to potential employers. The result is a resume or outreach message that looks like it’s coming from only a “just ok” candidate.  And in today’s job market, “just ok” is far from good enough. "
""
"So for those of you struggling to hear back about interviews or make it past the screening call, ask yourselves this: is the version of myself I’m putting out there - in terms of my materials or interview answers - top quartile quality? "
""
"If not, there isn’t a job search bot in the world that can help you until you lock down your messaging and get good at putting it forward."
""
"Luckily, we’re building a tool to help people close their self-advocacy skill gap at The ApplyAI. It’s free for another few months while we test new features, so sign up at www.theapply.ai and check it out. "
""
"P.S. Leave a comment or DM me if you want to be personally onboarded to the tool. I’ll show you exactly how to use our tool to take your job search/career growth journey to the next level."
""
"Like I said, it's a free tool, so I can't make it a sales pitch even if I tired!"
""
"Link to Aki's article: https://lnkd.in/grtkaN9W

Y’all I cannot stress this enough - ChatGPT is NOT enough."
""
"Used improperly, it might be your worst enemy."
""
"🚨 Recruiter talking to the Association for Talent Development: “After reviewing 50 applications, it was clear to her that more than 25 percent of the applicant pool had used ChatGPT to write their submissions."
""
"“They all started with the same four sentences,” she said as she shook her head."
""
"❌ “I had to disqualify them all.”"
""
"🗑️ Using ChatGPT is a “garbage in, garbage out” situation."
"When you give it limited context and simplistic prompts, you’ll always get generic content!"
""
"⬇️ For the record, it agrees with me"
""
"👉 We’ve built our product around the career coaching experience"
"👉 That helps our AI understand WHO YOU ARE"
"👉 Generated content is based on a genuine understanding of you (the context) + career coaching expertise (prompts)."
""
"🔜 If you’re ready for that brave new world, check us out at www.theapply.ai."
""
"🔗 Source: https://lnkd.in/eZancZbh

What a thrilling week at ASU+GSV Summit and ASU+GSV AIR Show!"
""
"From pounding the pavement to tell people about ApplyAI to pitching in the WGU Labs Accelerator Competition, there wasn't a dull moment. I was also filled with pride seeing Logan Currie crush our first-ever on-stage product demo"
""
"I saw a lot of cool companies and initiatives -- but I also noticed an anxious undertone when it comes to the future of work."
""
"There's genuine worry. "
""
"What does "hire-ability" look like in an AI world? How can we work towards that skills-based future without leaving a big swathe of people behind? "
""
"For me, everyone needs to answer three important questions:"
"1. How do I develop a rich "skills story"?"
"2. What kind of signaling are employers receptive to?"
"3. How can I focus my energy on sourcing the right opportunities?"
""
"I'm sure there are more important questions like these. "
""
"What do you think it will take to remain competitive in the years to come? "
""
"#ASUplusGSV #Edtech #ApplyAI #WGU

Authenticity isn't optional during a job search - you *will* be asked about who you are and what you've accomplished!"
""
"So do you really want to pin all your chances of getting a job on your BS-ing ability? "
""
"I don't think you need to"
""
"In my decade of helping people land their dream job or university offer, I have yet to meet someone who didn't have valuable things to say about themselves"
""
"It was my job to ask really good questions that reveal those details out and package that information in a compelling way that gets them noticed"
""
"Trust me - this is so much easier to do with someone's help than by yourself"
""
"That's why we've built an amazing tool that helps you think through your experiences and generates strong, personalized talking points that show off your best attributes "
""
"I've literally replaced myself with AI and I couldn't be happier - it's much better than I ever was AND it's available for free right now"
""
"If you or someone you know is ready to land their next big job opportunity, visit www.theapply.ai and become the world authority on yourself!

This shocks … no one. "
""
"The standout quote to me? "
""
"“Companies are swamped by applicants in part because many job seekers are newly relying on ChatGPT and automated bots to fire off large volumes of résumés, or using other shortcuts like LinkedIn’s “easy apply” button. ” "
""
"Basically: as a bad system gets worse, social capital becomes more important than ever. "
""
"Why am I worried for people?"
"- Networking is tough enough as it is - neurodiverse candidates, introverts, and people outside of traditionally “connected” circles will have it worst of all though"
"- The bar for getting a referral will inevitably rise. At McKinsey, I was asked a lot of questions about the person I was referring to the point where, if I barely knew someone or didn't have relevant info about them on-hand, the referral would have been buried by the system immediately"
""
"Luckily, the best solution is a simple one: double down on authenticity. "
""
"Take the time to understand your value proposition and practice communicating it to people who can help you in sufficient detail. They'll need the ammo if you want them to fight on your behalf!"
""
"It's as simple as that. "
""
"Don't rely on ChatGPT to do the heavy lifting - you'll spend just as much time trying to tease out something natural-sounding as you would have if you did it yourself, but without the added benefit of developing an increasingly important career skill. "
""
"That's why applicants that find success in this job market will excel at two things:"
"#1 Being "referral ready" at all times"
"#2 Being easy to refer on effectively"
""
"That’s the new bar.

Seeing as AI recommendations hold a lot of sway in the minds of hiring managers, there are two key takeaways we should remain mindful of as both job seekers. "
""
"First, companies are handing off more shortlisting responsibilities to digital systems, so there's a greater onus on you to optimize the materials you submit to that company and job description "
""
"Second, rejections are not a reflection of your worth as an professional - more often than not, there isn't a person on the other side passing judgement on you as applicant. "
""
"#Career #ATS #JobHunt

Being ghosted by a hiring manager undoubtedly sucks, but it's important that you not let it affect your drive to make the most of every opportunity. "
""
"That being said, a "thank you" note after an interview that includes some personalized takeaways from the conversation can help you stand out in a crowd. "
""
"If you don't want to be treated like just a number, set yourself apart from the other candidates through your actions. "
""
"#Career #Hiring #Leadership #PersonalGrowth

There are no end of articles like this one that all belabor the same basic point, and yet the point still doesn't seem to have gotten through to most people: Using ChatGPT poorly is just as bad for your job prospects - if not worse - than not using it at all. "
""
"People don't seem to get that AI isn't going to become magically creative on your behalf or figure out things about you out of the blue. "
""
"It is by definition constrained by what it knows (i. e. What you tell it). "
""
"What most people do is give it two pieces of info: the same job description as everyone else and their resume. Now granted everyone's resume is different, but as far as stores of information about you go, they aren't all that deep because all of your experiences are summarized into a dozen or so lines line. In the end, the bulk of what the AI has to draw from is still the JD. "
""
"So when you ask it to generate a cover letter or come up with new resume bullets, your stuff will come out sounding the same as everyone else's. "
""
"This problem is made worse when people give ChatGPT basic instructions that don't tell it what information to prioritize and or what structure to use, so it once again resorts to its base understanding of what to do. "
""
"The less context you give - for yourself, for your output, for your task - the more painfully generic the results. "
""
"For the record, that's why we've made discovery conversations with our AI career coach the centerpiece of our tool. To create truly great, personalized career content, you have to dig into your background in detail. That's what you need to do - whether that's by yourself, with a career coach, or using The ApplyAI. "
""
"#JobHunting #ChatGPT #Applying

Asking candidates to do complex tasks without some kind of compensation can be self-defeating. The very best candidates usually have enough options to forego time-consuming exercises for all but the most attractive of companies/roles while the ones that undertake the task may end up with a bitter taste in their mouths. I think this is especially true when it comes to things like design tasks, where candidates may feel like you're exploiting them for free labor. "
""
"Conversely, offering compensation - ideally at an hourly rate in line with the annual comp for the role in question - is a positive signal that applicants in our hiring process have called out as a reason why they had a higher degree of trust in our intentions at employers. I also think it leads to higher quality submissions, which in turn means you're getting a better assessment of their peak capabilities and, for the ones that convert, starting the working relationship on a positive note. "
""
"#Hiring #TalentPool #JobApplicants

There's some 𝐠𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐭 𝐚𝐝𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞 𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞 👇👇 "
""
"Networking is a crucial element of the job search - but also the easiest to ignore because of how uncomfortable the experience can be. "
""
"But *it* is worth it!"
""
"Now that application processes have become overly competitive, the best way to cut to the head of the queue is by getting referred by someone at the company. It won't work every time - but you only need it to work once to land a great job!"
""
"#JobSeeking #Networking #JobStrategy

This is a great hack for finding roles! "
""
"Because many companies don't want to pay to post these jobs on #LinkedIn, usually there are other ways job seekers can leverage the site's search functionalities to find postings. "
""
"Boolean operators can be an amazing search tool, one that narrows down results based on adaptive criteria. "

"#JobSeeking #Hiring #HR #Strategy

Every job seeker knows that it is incredibly annoying to type in the contents of one's resume into a form after uploading a document with that same information, but this seems to be something that won't go away anytime soon."
""
"Instead, I suggest job seekers find effective ways to do these tasks quickly and efficiently, either with automation or productivity tools. "
""
"My preferred solution? Keep everything you need to copy and paste in a **unformatted text** (.txt) file (Windows: Notepad, Mac: TextEdit). It's much easier to navigate than your resume and because it's unformatted, will always come out formatted appropriately in whatever box you paste it into. List out all 20 or so skills aligned with the job type you're applying for as well, Workday frequently asks you to manually input those. "
""
"#JobHunt #JobSeeking #Efficiency

No amount of enthusiasm will make up for a sh*t response to "You don't have enough industry experience.""
""
"Here are 3 simple but powerful replies to make you more convincing than 95% of career changers. "
""
"1. Rapidly climbing the learning curve is my superpower. Here’s an example.."
"2. Here's how my past roles translate directly into this new industry: "
"3. I've done my homework on your industry by…."
""
"Remember: you've got more transferable skills than you realize!

When asked why you want to join a company, resist the urge to say "Because you're hiring and I enjoy eating.""
""
"Instead, try: "
"⭐ "Your mission aligns perfectly with my passion for paying rent." "
"⭐ "I've always dreamed of having health insurance." "
"⭐ "Your value of 'work-life balance' really speaks to my core belief in 'life-work balance'."

I'll do this one more time for the people in the back. "
""
"Your resume bullets are probably costing you interviews right now..."
""
"Here's my bulletproof formula for crafting resume bullets that *actually* get noticed:"
""
"1. Impact first → Show exactly HOW you moved the needle ("Achieved 20% revenue increase", "improved user engagement by 12%")"
""
"2. Scale matters → What "level" are you operating at ("assisting C-suite", "supporting 12-person team", "completed in 2 months".)"
""
"3. Skills in action → Don't just list skills, show them in use ("Leveraged interpersonal communication", "Applied conflict resolution techniques")"
""
"4. Numbers, numbers, numbers → Quantify EVERYTHING (See examples from #1)"
""
"Remember: Vague bullets = instant rejection. Be specific or be skipped! 📝

This just in: Anthropic (yes, that one) is telling job applicants not to use AI tools in their applications. "
""
"Let me play that back just in case you missed that."
""
"An AI company that's actively working towards a future where AI transforms everything we do... doesn't want AI to transform applying to jobs… At their AI company. You can't make this up! "
""
"Here's what I find puzzling:"
"- They say AI will revolutionize work (except, apparently, for their own hiring process?)"
"- They say their ideal candidate will “be good to users” but then want said candidates to compete with one arm tied behind their backs."
"- They claim "AI-assisted communication" is unacceptable, but have left it so vaguely defined that applicants are left guessing where the line is."
""
"Here's the thing - as someone working in AI-enabled career tech myself, I *get* where the concerns are coming from. The Wild West of AI-generated applications isn't ideal for anyone."
""
"However, instead of a blanket "AI bad" policy, I believe the leading AI alignment company should do just that – lead on this issue by:"
"- Developing robust, AI-resistant hiring practices"
"- Acknowledging that purely "unassisted communication" is quickly becoming a relic of the past"
"- Establishing clear guidelines for what types of AI assistance are acceptable"
"- Pioneering best practices for ethical AI use in hiring"
""
"The real opportunity here isn't to ban AI outright - it's to lead the way in demonstrating what the responsible, effective use of AI in hiring looks like. "
""
"Let's go, Anthropic - light the way forward for all of us!"
""
"#AIHiring #FutureOfWork #Anthropic #Careerspan

🎯 STOP Being Humble - It's Killing Your Career Chances! "
""
"Here's a cold, hard fact: Modesty isn't serving you in your job search. If anything, it's probably holding you back from landing your desired role."
""
"Here's the 3-step framework that transforms self-promotion from awkward to authentic:"
""
"1. Deep Introspection 🤔"
"→ WHY? Because you can't tell your story if you don't know it yourself!"
"- What core problems have you solved?"
"- How did you specifically tackle challenges?"
"- What measurable impact did you create?"
"- What unique lessons did you learn?"
""
"2. Strategic Packaging 📦"
"→ WHY? Because raw facts need context to create impact!"
"• Which achievements showcase your growth?"
"• Which experiences highlight your leadership or initiative-taking?"
"• What are indicators of the scale of your involvement or impact?"
""
"3. Confident Delivery 🎤"
"→ WHY? Because confidence without arrogance is the sweet spot!"
"→ Present your value without apologizing"
"→ Focus on facts, not just feelings"
"→ Share stories, not just statements"
""
"🚨 Pro Tip: Reflect on specific anecdotes from your life - they're easier to analyze than abstract questions like "What are my strengths?""
""
"Remember: If you won't advocate for yourself, who will? Your achievements aren't going to speak for themselves - YOU need to give them a voice! "
""
"Interested in knowing how to translate your newfound self-advocacy to landing your desired job? "
""

"#CareerAdvice #PersonalBranding #JobSearch #ProfessionalDevelopment #CareersWithVrijen #Careerspan

🎯 The Art of Personal Marketing: How to Stand Out in Your Job Applications"
""
"Ever wonder why some candidates get picked over others with similar qualifications? "
""
"Truth bomb time: Unique has nothing to do with it - it's all about presenting the RIGHT combination of your qualities!"
""
"Let me break down my framework for crafting a compelling personal narrative that *actually* gets noticed:"
""
"1. Strategic Selection 🎯"
"→ WHY? Because scattershotting everything you've got doesn't work!"
"• Focus on 5-7 of your strongest qualities"
"• Prioritize depth over spread"
"• Target what matters most for THIS role"
""
"2. Deep Analysis of the Job 📋"
"→ WHY? Different companies value different things, even for similar roles!"
"• Decode the job description (first listed = most important)"
"• Match your capabilities to their needs"
"• Look for hidden opportunities beyond the basics"
""
"3. Value Proposition Formula 💫"
"→ WHY? Listing skills isn't enough - you need to show their application!"
"Your Trait/Skill + How You Developed It + Their Impact = Winning Formula"
""
"4. Growth Mindset Integration 🌱"
"→ WHY? You don't need to be perfect - you just need to show potential!"
"• Show learning capability"
"• Demonstrate adaptability"
"• Present development journey"
""
"🔑 Pro Tip: Don't try to be everything to everyone. Better to shine brightly in a few areas than dimly across many!"
""
"Remember: Your uniqueness isn't in any single trait - it's in your specific combination of experiences, skills, and attributes. Package them strategically!"
""
"What's your biggest challenge in presenting yourself? Drop a comment below!"
""
"#JobSearch #CareerAdvice #PersonalBranding #JobApplications #CareersWithVrijen #Careerspan

*Pastes in a job description*"
"*Pastes in their resume*"
"*Instructs ChatGPT to “tailor my resume”*"
"*Unknowingly lives to regret it* "
"🤦"
""
"The year is 2025, and if you’ve done anything resembling the above, you’ve unwittingly enlisted yourself in the ChatGPT Resume Clone Army - and martyred your job prospects in the process."
""
"I’ve seen countless people use this tactic and it never works out in their favor. Here’s why."
""
"This approach to resume customization fails miserably because:"
"➡️ ChatGPT is constrained to working with what's already on your resume"
"➡️ It can only swap words around or extend ideas in generic ways"
"➡️ It’s liable to making dangerous assumptions about your experiences, leaving you to waste time reversing those changes at best and getting caught out in an interview at worst"
"➡️ It produces resumes that are broadly indistinguishable and drive recruiters crazy!"
""
"Now let me be blunt: resumes are fundamentally terrible. They're one-dimensional representations of complex, nuanced careers. In an ideal world, they would be as extinct as dial-up internet. But at Careerspan, we’re pragmatists working within a broken system while pushing for better alternatives. And I firmly believe we’ve found one with our one-of-kind approach to resume tailoring."
""
"So what’s the right way to do things? Giving an AI your full backstory to work with - which is what we do 💪"
""
"This means:"
"👉 Starting with AI coaching conversations to uncover your FULL story BEFORE touching the resume"
"👉 Honing in on hidden achievements and forgotten accomplishments"
"👉 Structuring bullets using our proven Action-Impact-Scale-Skills framework"
""
"Our results speak for themselves: a 39% increase in ATS scores compared to original resumes and an 18% improvement over ChatGPT-tailored versions."
""
"This isn't just about swapping keywords like an overpaid thesaurus - it's about semantic optimization that analyzes job descriptions at a conceptual level and maps your capabilities onto requirements through pattern recognition."
""
"That’s why the real value of using Careerspan is getting help with articulating your unique professional story in the greatest amount of detail possible."
""
"So until we overthrow the yoke of resume tyranny altogether, I'm committed to making its burden that much easier."
""
"📣 Check out my full article on our blog in the comments below and like this post if you’re ready to defect from the ChatGPT clone army for good!"
""
"#AIResumes #CareerDevelopment #JobSearch #ResumeStrategy

The AI job application "arms race" is perpetuating a hiring doom loop that hurts EVERYONE."
""
"I just read a fascinating piece (link in the comments) by Michael Horn and Bob Moesta that perfectly captures what I've been seeing in the job market - we're trapped in a vicious cycle where AI tools are making things WORSE, not better."
""
"Here's the toxic pattern they've identified:"
"• Job seekers use AI to blast out hundreds of generic applications"
"• Employers get flooded with thousands of AI-generated resumes"
"• Companies either double-down on their own AI screening or abandon it entirely"
"• Hiring managers retreat to the safety of personal referrals"
"• Qualified candidates without connections get left behind"
""
"It's essentially mutually assured destruction for both sides of the hiring equation."
""
"In my opinion, the current generation of candidate-side AI tools are primarily fueling this problem because they focus on brute-force volume rather than meaningful personalization. "
""
"LazyApply, JobCopilot and their ilk are selling false hope - tricking job seekers into thinking they're making progress when they're actually just contributing to the noise."
""
"But here's where I think Careertech founders need to listen up: We need AI tools with INTEGRITY. Not tools that help you spam 500 employers, but tools that help you create genuinely tailored, authentic applications for roles you're actually qualified for."
""
"Until then, I'm with Horn and Moesta - invest your time in building real relationships instead of feeding the application machine."
""
"#AIHiring #JobSearch #NetworkingMatters #CareerTech

In the 1998 Tour de France, you had to go 18 riders down the finisher's list until you found one that *wasn't* doping."
""
"Was it because most cyclists are bad people?"
""
"While I'm tempted to say so as a New Yorker who's almost been run over a few times, the more honest answer is that they were part of a system that incentivized cheating."
""
"Sound familiar? Today's job market is failing the same way."
""
"When companies filter candidates by the hundreds using blunt instruments like ATS systems and proxies like pedigree, what do you expect candidates to do?"
""
"Juice their applications, GPT their cover letters, and inflate their backgrounds."
""
"Not because they're dishonest—rather, it's because they're being rational when faced with a flawed system, even if they're also inadvertently making things worse for everyone."
""
"Because when trust collapses, companies retreat further to "safe" signals like Ivy degrees and FAANG work experience."
""
"These proxies may signal competence, but competence alone does not a great hire make!"
""
"Put it this way: if you're starting a band, it's not always about finding the most technically gifted instrumentalist; rather, it's about finding someone with whom everyone jives musically."
""
"The best hires can do the job, but also so much more: they're the ones who step up when teammates struggle. Who mentor during coffee breaks. Who herald company values."
""
"That stuff doesn't show up on the resume -- but they are actually the overriding factors in job success. "
""
"46% of hires churn by the 18-month mark. 90% of the reason why is a values, mindset, or soft skills mismatch."
""
"So we have two roads ahead of us as employers."
""
"Path 1: Add more hoops. More tests. More paranoid screening. Watch as candidates get better at gaming those too."
""
"Path 2: Seek validated signals that actually matter - the stuff that can't be faked by ChatGPT or BSed in an interview."
""
"At Careerspan, we're betting everything on making Path 2 viable and scalable."
""
"Because when you assess people on the totality of what they bring to the table, square pegs end up in square holes, and everybody wins.

Would you rather barely survive a heart attack at 50 or get regular checkups starting at 30?"
""
"Because there's the rational choice....and then there's what most people do."
""
"Case in point, of the hundreds of job seekers I've spoken with over 2 years, *maybe* 5-10% do regular "career checkups." "
""
"Instead, they only act when stakes are high:"
"⁍ Activate networks when they desperately need warm connections"
"⁍ Update resumes after quitting, getting laid off, or emotionally checking out"
"⁍ Document achievements while stressed and scrambling"
""
"Here's the most future-proof career lesson you'll hear all week:"
""
"🚨 Those who maintain an ongoing record of achievements—who treat career development like preventative care—will dominate the AI era."
""
"Why? Because AI evaluates semantically. The richer your self-knowledge pool, the bigger your edge."
""
"So whether you’re journaling in a Google Doc or using our tool, start today and #futureproof your careerspan."

Dear job seekers who cold email:"
""
"We are no longer The ApplyAI"

🚨 Warning: This is NOT career advice. It's advice about career advice."
""
"Ever notice how career guidance can feel like a maze of contradictions? One expert says "job hop every two years," another insists "show loyalty.""
""
"Both can't be right... or can they?"
""
"Here's what I've learned after years of career coaching: Most folks haven't cultivated "advice literacy" - the skill of understanding why certain guidance exists and how to apply it to our unique situations."
""
"All conventional wisdom has implicit assumptions baked into it."
""
"For example, when someone says "follow your passion," they're assuming you have clarity about what that is, financial runway to pursue it, and that you're capable of translating passion into career success. "
""
"But what if you're supporting a family, discovering your interests through work, or building passion through mastery? "
""
"The advice isn't wrong - you need to understand it's really about finding work that energizes you, which can happen in many ways."
""
"Developing advice literacy means:"
"⚫️ Understanding the context behind guidance"
"⚫️ Recognizing what assumptions it makes about your situation"
"⚫️ Adapting the core insight to fit your actual constraints"
""
"This skill transforms how you level up. Instead of feeling confused by contradictory advice, you start seeing patterns."
""
"You realize that seemingly opposite guidance often targets different contexts, career stages, or goals."
""
"And once you develop this lens, every piece of career content becomes more valuable--even when it isn't immediately relevant."
""
"Moral of the story: Stop blindly collecting advice and start thoughtfully incorporating it into a mental model."
""
"🧐 What contradictory career advice has left you scratching your head? Let's see if we can resolve the paradox in the comments!

We try and build a network when we should have one warmed up and ready to leverage. "
""

"👉 Conversations that actually mattered."

""

The Auto-Applycolypse Will Doom Us All

The Auto-Applycolypse Will Doom Us All

Created on 2025-06-24 15:25

Published on 2025-06-24 15:45

Auto-apply this, auto-apply that....why don’t you stop auto-f*cking your chances of getting a job by using terrible AI tools that don’t have your best interests at heart. 

Don’t take it from me – The NYT thinks the same (article in the comments), as do the dozens of Chief HR Officers and Talent leaders I’ve spoken to in the last two months. 

All auto-apply tools offer is the false comfort of "number go up". They exploit your desperation with vanity metrics. "Congrats! You've applied to 847 jobs this week!". 

What these companies aren’t telling you - because they know you won’t check - is that their AI agents are just as likely to put in an app for a project manager at Meta as they are forklift operator in Fargo. 

They rely on a well-known bias call "metric fixation" and boy do they know you'll fixate. 

Never mind that you're getting a 0.2% response rate - because quantity is obviously ≠ quality - and then internalizing that as you sucking when it’s just your approach that sucks. 

Auto-apply companies know this - and they literally don't care. 

You’re playing the wrong “numbers game”

“But V - it’s a numbers game…right?”, you ask uncomfortably as the realization that you were just repeating a meaningless cliche dawns on you. 

Well, yes and no.

Yes - the number of shots on goal generally correlates with your likelihood to score EXCEPT if all you’re doing is yeeting the ball in any given direction 

A 2023 academic study (Campero & Kacperczyk) involving over 140,000 applications found that even minor tailoring (adjusting job titles or keywords) produced measurable improvements in interview rates—outperforming even the effect of internal referrals. Our own internal study found that semantically tailored resumes averaged an ATS score lift of 18% over generic resumes. 

You’re not sticking it to the system, you’re gumming up the works

Also, and I hate to be that guy, but you (yes, you specifically) using auto-apply is making it worse for EVERYONE

Every spammed application gives employers another excuse to raise barriers. To add more hoops. To retreat further behind referral walls. Heck, 70% of jobs never even end up on a public job board—they're filled through the "hidden job market" of connections and word-of-mouth, now more than ever. 

One last thing: Employers, you brought this upon yourselves 

You spent decades filtering candidates through tired signals—prestigious degrees, brand-name employers, keyword bingo—rather than seeking deeper understanding of the humans worth considering. 

You set up a candidate evaluation paradigm that that is superficial at best and outright inequitable at worst because “there’s plenty of fish in the sea” – why get better at picking when you can just be pickier. 

You automated away humanity, then made the surprised pikachu face when candidates fight fire with fire. 

Don’t expect sympathy from candidates - instead, I advise you to get better at selecting for human traits and not the presence of keywords.  

So where do go from here? Here’s my two cents. Candidates - Tell authentic stories. Make real connections. Don’t come off as a bot; show up as a real person. And for the love of all that is holy, stop spamming your resume. Because auto-apply isn't your secret weapon—it's career self-sabotage wrapped in Silicon Valley marketing.

Employers - take a long, hard look at your hiring process. Find ways to separate the wheat from the chaff on a more nuanced, human-centric basis. 

Because avoiding mutually assured exasperation will require both sides to de-escalate.

Don't Get Spooked by Ghost Jobs: A Smarter Approach to Your Job Search

Don't Get Spooked by Ghost Jobs: A Smarter Approach to Your Job Search

Created on 2025-04-22 12:58

Published on 2025-04-22 13:02

If you've been job hunting lately, you've probably experienced this: You spend hours tailoring your resume and crafting the perfect cover letter for what looks like your dream role. Days pass. Weeks pass. Nothing but crickets.

It's not just you. You've likely encountered what we in the industry call a "ghost job" - a position advertised with zero intent to hire. According to recent data from Greenhouse, these phantom postings make up a staggering 18-22% of all job listings. The Wall Street Journal confirms this troubling trend, noting that roughly 1 in 5 online job ads may be ghost jobs.

So why do companies post jobs they have no intention of filling? Sometimes they're building a candidate pipeline for future openings. Other times, they're signaling growth to investors or employees. Some are holding out for that mythical "unicorn" candidate, while others are simply complying with public-posting requirements after making an internal hire.

Whatever the reason, the impact on job seekers is real. Three in five candidates suspect they've encountered ghost jobs, leading to wasted time, diminished confidence, and increasingly desperate tactics.

How Should You Respond?

Rather than giving up or resorting to the mass-application approach (which only dilutes your personal brand), here's how to navigate this haunted job market:

Adopt a tiered effort strategy. Not all job postings deserve the same level of attention. Save your energy for high-alignment roles while using a more streamlined approach for everything else.

When you find a role that truly excites you, go all in. This means a tailored resume, customized cover letter, and strategic networking outreach, all submitted as quickly as possible after the job is posted.

Learn to spot the ghosts. Check how long the posting has been active, whether it has a clear timestamp, and if it appears on the company's own website. Favor platforms that verify their job listings.

Speed + personalization is the winning formula. When you do find a legitimate opportunity, being both fast and distinctive gives you the best chance of standing out.

Coming Soon: Tools to Combat the Ghost Job Epidemic

At Careerspan, we've been working on a solution to help you navigate this challenging landscape, set to launch next month.

Our new JD Gut Check will evaluate JDs based on your true alignment -- not just based on keyword or hard skills basis, but also your soft-skills alignment, culture fit, etc., helping you prioritize where to invest your valuable time.

For those promising opportunities that pass the authenticity check, our Smart Application Suite will generate professionally tailored materials in minutes. This means you can submit high-quality, personalized applications quickly while maintaining the unique voice that makes you stand out from the crowd.

The Job Search Shouldn't Feel Like a Maze

The real edge in today's job market isn't volume—it's precision and speed. Knowing which roles are genuine and hitting them hard with materials that resonate will serve you better than scattering generic applications across the internet.

Stop wasting precious time on positions that don't actually exist. The job market is challenging enough without chasing phantoms. With the right strategy—and soon, the right tools—you can focus your energy where it matters most.

Stay tuned for our launch next month. Your job search is about to get a whole lot more effective!
